title: tilia paucicostata subsp. yunnanensis
intro: Tilia paucicostata subsp. yunnanensis, also known as the Yunnan linden tree, is a subspecies of the Tilia paucicostata plant, native to the Yunnan province in China. The bark of this plant contains salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin and is known for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The medicinal properties of Tilia paucicostata subsp. yunnanensis have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, with its health benefits also extending to reducing stress and promoting sleep. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
